title: Scented beeswax heart ornaments
subtitle: Small moulded beeswax hearts scented with lavender, used as drawer fresheners or moth deterrents
excerpt: These are not burning candles. They are solid, fragrant ornaments made from beeswax and lavender essential oil, poured into small heart moulds and hung or placed in wardrobes, drawers, or linen cupboards. Beeswax releases its fragrance slowly at room temperature.
sourceNotes: Beeswax ornament method from standard craft and homesteading references. Lavender essential oil moth-deterrent properties referenced from textile conservation literature.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Leftover wax re-melts cleanly.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Can be made months in advance. The scent continues to release for 6 to 12 months.
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt beeswax to 70 degrees Celsius. Add essential oil at 65 degrees Celsius.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months of scent release. The wax itself lasts indefinitely but the lavender scent diminishes. Refresh by adding a few drops of lavender essential oil to the surface and allowing to absorb.

glossaryTerms.definition: A natural wax produced by honeybees with a melting point of 62 to 65 degrees Celsius and a characteristic honey scent. At room temperature it releases fragrance slowly, making it ideal for scented ornaments.

The proportion of essential oil added to a wax or base. For solid beeswax ornaments used at room temperature rather than burned, a rate of 2 to 3% is sufficient for a noticeable scent without causing the oil to bleed from the surface.

A material or scent used to discourage clothes moths from laying eggs on natural fibres. Lavender, cedar, and rosemary are traditional plant-based deterrents. They deter rather than kill moths and require refreshing when the scent fades.

content.text: These beeswax ornaments are not candles: they are never lit. They work as scented decorations and practical
for the wardrobe or linen cupboard.
releases fragrance gently at room temperature without any heat source. The
is 2 to 3% of the wax weight. Higher rates cause the oil to bleed from the surface onto fabrics. Shelf life is 12 months of effective scent release; the wax itself is indefinitely stable.

Melt beeswax in a double boiler to 70 degrees Celsius. Remove from heat and allow to cool to 65 degrees Celsius. Add lavender essential oil and stir gently for 1 minute. Pour into the heart silicone mould cavities, filling to the rim.

After approximately 10 to 15 minutes, when the wax is firm but not fully set, use a toothpick or skewer to make a small hole near the top of each heart for threading the ribbon. If you miss the window, a warm skewer can be pushed through the fully set wax. Leave to set completely for at least 1 hour. Pop out of the mould, thread with ribbon, and hang.

These ornaments release scent for approximately 12 months. When the lavender fragrance fades, add 2 to 3 drops of lavender essential oil to the surface of each ornament and allow to absorb for an hour before returning to the wardrobe. The wax base can be refreshed this way indefinitely. Store in a cool place if not in use; heat will cause the beeswax to soften and may mark fabric.
